Mandatory: is to be interpreted as described in IETF RFC 2119
A Network Connection connects the Client to the Server
A Network Connection provides the means to send an ordered, lossless, stream of bytes in both directions
When an Application Message is transported by MQTT it contains payload data, a Quality of Service (QoS), a collection of Properties, and a Topic Name
A Client opens the Network Connection to the Server
A Server accepts Network Connections from Clients
A Subscription comprises a Topic Filter and a maximum QoS
A Subscription is associated with a single Session
A Session can contain more than one Subscription
Each Subscription within a Session has a different Topic Filter
A Shared Subscription comprises a Topic Filter and a maximum QoS
An Application Message that matches a Shared Subscription is only sent to the Client associated with one of these Sessions
A Session can subscribe to more than one Shared Subscription
A Wildcard Subscription is a Subscription with a Topic Filter containing one or more wildcard characters
The Wildcard Subscription allows the subscription to match more than one Topic Name
The label attached to an Application Message which is matched against the Subscriptions known to the Server
An expression contained in a Subscription to indicate an interest in one or more topics
A Topic Filter can include wildcard characters
A control packet that cannot be parsed according to this specification
An error that is detected after the packet has been parsed and found to contain data that is not allowed by the protocol or is inconsistent with the state of the Client or Server
An Application Message which is published by the Server after the Network Connection is closed in cases where the Network Connection is not closed normally
The set of Unicode Control Codes and Unicode Noncharacters which should not be included in a UTF-8 Encoded String
Prohibited: should not be included in a UTF-8 Encoded String

Mandatory: It is a Protocol Error to include the Session Expiry Interval more than once
The Client and Server MUST store the Session State after the Network Connection is closed if the Session Expiry Interval is greater than 0
Recommended: The Client should always use the Session Present flag in the CONNACK to determine whether the Server has a Session State for this Client
Clients should only connect with a long Session Expiry Interval if they intend to reconnect to the Server at some later point in time
When a Client has determined that it has no further use for the Session it should disconnect with a Session Expiry Interval set to 0

MUST NOT: MUST NOT send any more PUBLISH packets with QoS > 0

Mandatory: MUST use one of the DISCONNECT Reason Code values

mandatory: MUST respond with a SUBACK packet
MUST have the same Packet Identifier
MUST replace that existing Subscription with a new Subscription
MUST NOT be lost due to replacing the Subscription
MUST handle that packet as if it had received a sequence of multiple SUBSCRIBE packets
MUST contain a Reason Code for each Topic Filter/Subscription Option pair
MUST either show the maximum QoS that was granted for that Subscription or indicate that the subscription failed
MUST be the minimum of the QoS of the originally published message and the Maximum QoS granted by the Server
are removed from the Server's Session State when the Server receives an UNSUBSCRIBE packet

are removed from the Server's Session State when the Server receives a SUBSCRIBE packet from the Client for the same Topic Filter but with a different Subscription Identifier or with no Subscription Identifier
are removed from the Server's Session State when the Server sends Session Present 0 in a CONNACK packet
is not allowed to revert to the old identifier after it has sent a PUBLISH packet containing the new one

is the responsibility of the Responder to make sure

MUST deliver the message to the Client respecting the maximum QoS of all the matching subscriptions
MUST send those Subscription Identifiers in the message which is published as the result of the subscriptions
MUST include in the PUBLISH packet the Subscription Identifiers for all matching subscriptions which have Subscription Identifiers
MUST send, in each of them, the Subscription Identifier of the matching subscription if it has a Subscription Identifier
MUST NOT contain a Subscription Identifier
MUST NOT send more than Receive Maximum QoS 1 and QoS 2 PUBLISH packets for which the Client has not received PUBACK, PUBCOMP, or PUBREC with a Reason Code of 128 or greater from the Server
MUST NOT delay the sending of any packets other than PUBLISH packets due to having sent Receive Maximum PUBLISH packets without receiving acknowledgements for them

PROTOCOL ERROR: It is a Protocol Error for a PUBLISH packet to contain any Subscription Identifier other than those received in SUBSCRIBE packet which caused the PUBLISH packet to flow
A Topic Alias value of 0 or greater than the Maximum Topic Alias is a Protocol Error
If the packet has a zero length Topic Name field it is a Protocol Error

checking that the Disallowed Unicode code points are not used
Validating UTF-8 Encoded strings avoids implementation difficulties
protects applications from having to process Disallowed Unicode code points
Validating that these code points are not used removes some security exposures
The Server is allowed to not ever send a Server Reference
the Client is allowed to ignore a Server Reference
Prohibited: A name enclosed by square brackets cannot contain the right square bracket ( ] ) character
Recommended: It is recommended that each reference consists of a name optionally followed by a colon and a port number

MUST: MUST assign an unused Packet Identifier when the sender has a new Application Message to publish
MUST send a PUBLISH packet containing this Packet Identifier with QoS 2 and DUP flag set to 0
MUST treat the PUBLISH packet as unacknowledged until the sender has received the corresponding PUBREC packet from the receiver
MUST send a PUBREL packet when the sender receives a PUBREC packet from the receiver with a Reason Code value less than 0x80
MUST contain the same Packet Identifier as the original PUBLISH packet
MUST treat the PUBREL packet as unacknowledged until the sender has received the corresponding PUBCOMP packet from the receiver
MUST respond with a PUBREC containing the Packet Identifier from the incoming PUBLISH packet, having accepted ownership of the Application Message
MUST treat any subsequent PUBLISH packet that contains that Packet Identifier as being a new Application Message
MUST acknowledge any subsequent PUBLISH packet with the same Packet Identifier by sending a PUBREC
MUST NOT cause duplicate messages to be delivered to any onward recipients in this case
MUST respond to a PUBREL packet by sending a PUBCOMP packet containing the same Packet Identifier as the PUBREL
MUST treat any subsequent PUBLISH packet that contains that Packet Identifier as being a new Application Message
MUST continue the QoS 2 acknowledgement sequence even if the receiver has applied message expiry
MUST NOT: MUST NOT re-send the PUBLISH once the sender has sent the corresponding PUBREL packet
MUST NOT apply Message expiry if a PUBLISH packet has been sent

Mandatory: MUST be sent in WebSocket binary data frames
MUST close the Network Connection
MUST NOT assume that MQTT Control Packets are aligned on WebSocket frame boundaries
MUST include mqtt in the list of WebSocket Sub Protocols the Client offers
MUST be mqtt
Prohibited: MUST NOT assume that MQTT Control Packets are aligned on WebSocket frame boundaries
